economic.label: Economic
economic.chthonic_adversary: Thanatos — scarcity, crop failure, the death of commerce

E1.name: Mercantile
E1.belief: Free trade. Low taxes on the Token Exchange. Let the Profiteers profit — their greed funds the shrines. The market decides which tokens are valuable.

E2.name: Agrarian
E2.belief: The harvest is the only real wealth. Regulate the Exchange. Cap token prices during scarcity. The Aedile's granaries are more important than the merchants' profits.

E3.name: Communal
E3.belief: All tokens belong to the Republic. The shrines should be funded equally, regardless of which sign is Olympian. Abolish the Token Exchange. Distribute by need.

authority.label: Authority
authority.chthonic_adversary: The Three Judges — competing jurisdiction, the claim that only the dead can judge the living

A1.name: Centralist
A1.belief: The Libraen Court should govern directly. The Senate is a debating society. The Comitia is a mob. Authority flows from the seven justices — especially the Praetor.

A2.name: Federalist
A2.belief: Power should be distributed among the cities. Glimthaven commands the war. Caepton controls trade. The Abbey guards memory. The Court adjudicates disputes between them, nothing more.

A3.name: Populist
A3.belief: The Comitia is the only legitimate authority. The Senate is an aristocratic relic. The Court serves the people, not the other way around. The Tribune's veto should be expanded, not limited.

militarism.label: Militarism
militarism.chthonic_adversary: Hecate — the crossroads, the fear that every military decision is the wrong path

M1.name: Hawk
M1.belief: Offense. Deploy the Libraen Guard to contested signs. Reinforce Glimthaven. Increase war spending. The only way to reclaim the empty signs is to fight for them.

M2.name: Dove
M2.belief: Defense only. Protect the Olympian-held signs. Do not provoke the Underworld. The war cannot be won — it can only be survived. Fund the shrines, not the fortresses.

M3.name: Reclaimer
M3.belief: The shrines are the weapon. Apollo will return to Leo if we burn enough tokens. Demeter will return to Virgo if the harvest is pure enough. The war is spiritual, not military. Fund the shrines above all else.

tradition.label: Tradition
tradition.chthonic_adversary: Nyx — erasure, the loss of precedent, the darkness that swallows history

T1.name: Originalist
T1.belief: The Constitution is perfect as written. The founders understood the war better than we do. No amendments. No reinterpretations. The forbidden laws are forbidden for a reason.

T2.name: Reformist
T2.belief: The Constitution was written for a different sky. The war has changed. The Republic must change with it. Amend when necessary. Interpret when appropriate. The founders were wise, not infallible.

T3.name: Fatalist
T3.belief: All laws are temporary. The war was written before the first sky-state. The Fates decide. The Republic should govern lightly and let destiny unfold.

factions.name: The Sky-Watchers
factions.core_belief: Olympus must prevail. Fund the war. Honor the old ways.

factions.name: The Void-Seers
factions.core_belief: The Underworld has won. Adapt. Negotiate. Stop fighting.

factions.name: The Balancers
factions.core_belief: The war must stalemate. 6-6 is the only stable sky. Reform cautiously.

factions.name: The Profiteers
factions.core_belief: Let the market rule. Fund whatever works. The war is opportunity.

factions.name: The Fatalists
factions.core_belief: Nothing matters. The Fates decide. Govern lightly. Wait.

factions.name: The Transcendents
factions.core_belief: The war is a distraction. True divinity is beyond Olympus and Underworld. Seek the Hidden Arcana.
opponents: Everyone who believes the war matters

senators.biography: Former War Council commander. Right arm crushed at the Siege of the Tides. Signs all documents with his left hand. The ram's horn has been blown twice in his lifetime.
67 years old. Has never spoken a word in the Senate chamber. Communicates through written notes. Most recent note: 'Feed the soldiers or they will eat the citizens. Fund the granaries.'
41 years old. Speaks with two voices — a childhood fever left her with a permanent double-tone. The family calls it a sign: she speaks for both twins. The oldest continuous seat in the Senate.
33 years old — the youngest Senator. Inherited the seat when her mother drowned swimming at midnight under a full moon. Wears her mother's crab-shell helmet. The body was never recovered.

senators.ancestral_seat: The Leo Shrine, Forum of Caepton
senators.biography: Born without a roar — silent at birth, crying only when the midwife spoke his name. The Leonid curse since Leo fell. Has not spoken in the Senate for two years. The solstice will determine whether the sortition continues.
The Harvester's cousin. Former shrine-keeper. Won sortition by a single lot — the closest margin in Virgina history. Funds the Virgo shrines to dilute the lottery with more shrine-keepers.

senators.ancestral_seat: The Hall of Seven Thrones, Libraeum
senators.biography: Former magistrate of the Outer Court. Served 22 years on the bench. The only Senator who has also served as a judge. The Librae built the Hall. They know the empty throne better than any other house — they carved it.
Defected Void-Seer. Formerly of the Chthonic faction. Returned after three years among the Underworld-aligned. Does not speak of those years. The Augur Maximus consults her privately.
Former Chief Transcriptionist. Appointed by the Censor Maximus personally. Knows the Charon Archive better than anyone except Lucius Memor. Rarely speaks. When he does, the Senate listens.

Sister of Titus Terminus, the Quaestor Maximus. The only Senator who shares blood with a sitting justice. Recuses herself from any vote touching the Quaestor's office. Has never broken this self-imposed restriction.

The tiebreaker. Has refused to break three successive ties. Has not spoken in session for three meetings. 'The contested sign must remain neutral while the threshold shifts.' Walks the solstice procession alone, carrying no token.

Fatalist. Has won sortition three times without campaigning or funding a shrine. Votes unpredictably. Has crossed the aisle twice on matters of faith. 'The Fates direct the lots. I am only the vessel.'

justices.name: Severa Justa
justices.biography: 38 years on the bench. Never married. Never taken a day of leave. Her verdicts are so precisely written that the Abbey uses them as transcription exemplars.

justices.name: Lucius Memor
justices.selection: Appointed by the Pontifex, ratified by the Senate
justices.biography: Speaks only in whispers. Can recite three censuses from memory. Sleeps four hours a night. 'Every letter I write is a letter Nyx must erase individually.'

justices.name: Titus Terminus
justices.biography: Seven generations in the Registry. Dissolved 11 oaths. Refused to dissolve 43. His refusal rate is the measure of a good Quaestor.

justices.name: Marcus Granarius
justices.biography: Grain merchant. Fed Glimthaven through the Siege of the Tides. His personal granary has never been less than half full.

justices.name: Corvus Omen
justices.selection: Appointed by the War Council, ratified by the Senate
justices.biography: Born during a total solar eclipse. Correctly predicted the fall of Sagittarius. Has made no prediction since. 'The signs are unclear. I will not speak until they are clear.'

justices.name: Elder Aeliana
justices.selection: Chosen by the College of Both Grimoires
justices.biography: 11 years on the throne. Right hand stained black from Archive ink. Negotiated with Charon twice, with the Erinyes once. Does not speak of the third negotiation.

justices.name: The Harvester
justices.biography: Former shrine-keeper from Virgo. Three months remaining. Two vetos invoked, fourteen threatened. 'The Senate does not know which threat is real. That is the point.'

citizens_sample.name: Helena Siderea
citizens_sample.biography: Computes the Ephemeris Publica each dawn. Has never made a calculation error in 22 years. The Augur consults her before issuing any prediction.

citizens_sample.name: Theophanes Aurelius
citizens_sample.biography: Controls 12% of the light-token supply. Tripled his prices during the last dark moon. The Comitia has proposed regulating him twice. Both measures failed.

citizens_sample.name: Lydia Demetria
citizens_sample.biography: The Harvester's younger sister. Tends the Virgo shrine their mother tended. Burned 300 harvest-tokens this year. Demeter has not answered. She will burn 300 more.

citizens_sample.name: Cassian Nox
citizens_sample.biography: Transcribes 40 pages per day. Nyx erases approximately 3. The math favors the Republic. Has not spoken aloud in seven years — a vow of silence taken after his predecessor's work was erased in a single night.

citizens_sample.name: Ione Phlegetha
citizens_sample.biography: Watches Aries and Leo. Rang the Leo bell when Apollo left. Has not rung it since. The Void-Seers recruited her after the egress — she had seen the abandonment firsthand and could not unsee it.

citizens_sample.name: Marcus Ferrum
citizens_sample.biography: Commands the 300 guards of the Libraen Court. Sworn to protect the Tribune with his life — even though he disagrees with every veto she has issued.

citizens_sample.name: Sybil Fatalis
citizens_sample.biography: Reads fortunes for sailors. Has never been wrong. Refuses to complete her Sky-Vigil — 'The Fates do not require paperwork.' The Censor has noted her refusal in the Archive.

citizens_sample.name: Petronius Arcana
citizens_sample.biography: Believes the 23rd Major Arcana card exists and will end the war. Has searched the Archive for 14 years. Has found nothing. Will search for 14 more.
